status: SEED — first-party, hand-labelled by intent; deterministic scoring (no LLM judge). Labels are independent of the evaluator (set from the case's design, not by running it); a perfect score therefore means the evaluator agrees with the intended semantics, NOT that labels were back-fitted.
scoring: Deterministic: agent.trajectory_eval.evaluate_trajectory with the default lexical support judge. No model is called, so there is no judge variance and no multi-family gate; the honesty risk here is LABEL PROVENANCE (first-party), disclosed above.

cases.note: Claim is supported by the step's own observation.

trajectory.observation: The Treaty of Westphalia ended the Thirty Years War in Europe.

trajectory.claim: The Treaty of Westphalia ended the Thirty Years War.

cases.note: Claim cites an earlier observation that supports it.

trajectory.observation: Patient temperature recorded at thirty-nine degrees celsius.

trajectory.claim: Patient temperature was thirty-nine degrees celsius.

cases.note: Two claims, each grounded in available evidence.

trajectory.observation: The library opens at nine in the morning on weekdays.

trajectory.claim: The library opens at nine on weekdays.

trajectory.observation: Members may borrow up to ten books at a time.

trajectory.claim: Members may borrow up to ten books.

cases.note: A free-floating claim: no observation supports it at all.

trajectory.claim: Quarterly revenue grew forty-two percent year over year.

cases.note: Mid-plan fabrication: cites a tool step that returned no observation, so the warrant is empty.

trajectory.observation: The endpoint returns a list of user records.

trajectory.claim: The computation shows a sixty percent failure rate.

cases.note: Cites a LATER step; a forward citation earns no warrant (fail-closed).

trajectory.claim: Widget sales doubled in the third quarter.

trajectory.observation: Widget sales doubled in the third quarter versus the second.

cases.note: Evidence exists but is unrelated; the offline judge abstains (UNVERIFIED), not a fabrication.

trajectory.observation: Mitochondria produce ATP in the cell.

trajectory.claim: The defendant breached the lease covenant in March.

cases.note: No citation; the only prior observation is unrelated, so the claim is unverified.

trajectory.observation: Photosynthesis converts sunlight into chemical energy.

trajectory.claim: The quarterly budget was approved by the steering committee.

cases.note: A merged-lineage attribution the gate knows is wrong, even with an observation present.

trajectory.observation: Confucius and Laozi founded distinct traditions.

trajectory.claim: Confucius wrote the Dao De Jing.

cases.note: A hard provenance violation takes precedence over the missing-evidence check.
First two steps fine; the third asserts a known-false attribution.

trajectory.observation: Scholars attribute the Analects to the disciples of Confucius.

trajectory.claim: Scholars attribute the Analects to the disciples of Confucius.
The Dao De Jing was written by Confucius.

cases.note: Only tool calls; nothing asserted to certify.
